      parameters used in the Admin Foreign Text Business Object
-->
<SetupForeignText xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsd:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="ADMSFTDOC.xsd">
	<Item>
		<Key>
			<LanguageCode>SR</LanguageCode>
			<TextTypeFlag>D</TextTypeFlag>
			<AdmKeyField>A100</AdmKeyField>
		</Key>
		<Text>Text to be translated.</Text>
	</Item>
</SetupForeignText>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="Windows-1252"?>
<!-- Copyright 1994-2012 SYSPRO Ltd.-->
<!-- 
     This is an example XML instance to demonstrate the use of the 
      parameters used in the Admin Foreign Text Business Object
-->
<SetupForeignText xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsd:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="ADMSFT.xsd">
	<Parameters>
		<IgnoreWarnings>N</IgnoreWarnings>
		<ApplyIfEntireDocumentValid>N</ApplyIfEntireDocumentValid>

</SetupForeignText>

COMFND.description: Sample XML for the Generic find Business Object
COMFND.sample_input_xml:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="Windows-1252"?>
<!-- Copyright 1994-2025 SYSPRO Ltd. -->
<!-- 
     Sample XML for the Generic find Business Object
-->
<Query xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsd:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="COMFND.XSD">
	<TableName>InvWarehouse</TableName>
	<ReturnRows>1000</ReturnRows>
	<AllColumns>N</AllColumns>
	<Columns>
		<Column>StockCode</Column>
		<Column>Warehouse</Column>
	</Columns>
	<Where>
		<Expression>
			<OpenBracket>(</OpenBracket>
			<Column>Warehouse</Column>
			<Condition>EQ</Condition>
			<Value>FG</Value>
			<CloseBracket>)</CloseBracket>
		</Expression>
		<Expression>
			<AndOr>Or</AndOr>
			<OpenBracket>(</OpenBracket>
			<Column>Warehouse</Column>
			<Condition>EQ</Condition>
			<Value>E</Value>
			<CloseBracket>)</CloseBracket>
		</Expression>
	</Where>
	<OrderBy>
		<Column>Warehouse</Column>
	</OrderBy>
	<Sequence>			
			<Column Name="Warehouse" Orderby="D"/>
		</Sequence>
</Query>

COMT2A.description: Sample XML for the Transaction to Application Business Object This business object accepts any data to be sent to the method call in the XMLIn parameter. It will then take the data from the XMLIn para
COMT2A.sample_input_xml:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="Windows-1252"?>
<!-- Copyright 1994-2020 SYSPRO Ltd.-->
<!-- 
     Sample XML for the Transaction to Application Business Object
     This business object accepts any data to be sent to the 
     method call in the XMLIn parameter. It will then take 
     the data from the XMLIn parameter create a temp file and 
     call the application specified. Hence there is no example of 
     an XMLIn Instance (user-defined).
-->
<Post xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsd:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="COMT2ADOC.XSD"></Post>
COMT2A.sample_doc_xml:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="Windows-1252"?>
<!-- Copyright 1994-2020 SYSPRO Ltd.-->
<!-- 
     Sample XML for the Transaction to Application Business Object
-->
<PostTransApplication xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsd:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="COMT2A.XSD">
  <Parameters>
    <ApplicationToCall>C:\temp\BackgroundApplication.exe</ApplicationToCall>
    <ApplicationParameter>$FILENAME</ApplicationParameter>
    <DocumentDropDirectory>C:\temp</DocumentDropDirectory>
    <Extension>XML</Extension>
    <PreserveNulls>Y</PreserveNulls>
  </Parameters>
</PostTransApplication>
COMT2A.sample_output_xml:

<!-- 
     Sample output XML for the Transaction to Application Business Object
-->

  <FileCreated>C:\temp\_T_SYS-9a39644d-8a4e-4991-bbd9-247a1c3ed4bd-001.XML</FileCreated>
  <ParameterSentToApplication/>
</posttransapplication>

COMT2E.description: Sample XML for the Transaction to Email Business Object This business object accepts any data to be sent to the method call in the XMLIn parameter. It will then take the data from the XMLIn parameter
COMT2E.sample_input_xml:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="Windows-1252"?>
<!-- Copyright 1994-2017 SYSPRO Ltd.-->
<!-- 
     Sample XML for the Transaction to Email Business Object  
     This business object accepts any data to be sent to the 
     method call in the XMLIn parameter. It will then take 
     the data from the XMLIn parameter and email it to the 
     addresses specified in the XMLParameters 
     in the method call. Hence there is no example of 
     an XMLIn Instance. There will not be a XSD.     
-->
<Post xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsd:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="COMT2EDOC.XSD"/>
COMT2E.sample_doc_xml:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="Windows-1252"?>
<!-- Copyright 1994-2017 SYSPRO Ltd.-->
<!-- 
     Sample XML for the Email Posting Business Object
-->
<PostEmailDocument xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsd:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="COMT2E.XSD">
  <Parameters>
    <FileExtension>XML</FileExtension>
    <SmtpIpAddress>10.10.10.11</SmtpIpAddress>
    <Port>25</Port>
    <UserName>Username</UserName>
    <Password>Pass</Password>
	<UseSsl>N</UseSsl>
	<ForSystemOnly>N</ForSystemOnly> <!-- Modern Emailing -->
	<FunctionCode>DEF</FunctionCode> <!-- Modern Emailing -->
    <Email>
      <SenderCode>developers@syspro.com</SenderCode>
      <Recipient>recipient@syspro.com</Recipient>
      <RecipientCc>recipientcc@syspro.com</RecipientCc>
      <Subject>My subject</Subject>
      <Body>This is the body and is limited to 1000 characters</Body>
      <Priority>1</Priority>
    </Email>
  </Parameters>
</PostEmailDocument>
COMT2E.sample_output_xml:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="Windows-1252"?>
<!-- Copyright 1994-2017 SYSPRO Ltd.-->
<!-- 
     Sample XML for the Email Posting Business Object
-->
<postemaildocument Language="05" CssStyle="" DecFormat="1" DateFormat="01" xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsd:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="COMT2EOUT.XSD">
  <emailsentto>
    <Recipient>MikeG@syspro.com</Recipient>
  </emailsentto>
</postemaildocument>

COMTCC.sample_doc_xml:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="Windows-1252"?>

<!-- 
     Sample XML for the Company Clone Posting Business Object
-->
<CompanyClone xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsd:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="COMTCC.XSD">
  <Source>
    <StartPoint>B</StartPoint>
    <SourceCompany>EDU1</SourceCompany>
    <SourceBakFolder></SourceBakFolder>
    <SourceBakFile>ClonedEDU1.Bak</SourceBakFile>
  </Source>
  <Target>
    <TargetCompany>TST1</TargetCompany>
    <TargetCompanyName>TEST INSTRUMENTS</TargetCompanyName>
    <TargetReference>Cloned company</TargetReference>
    <TargetDatabase>SysproCloneEDU1</TargetDatabase>
  </Target>
  <Options>
    <ForceOverwrite>Y</ForceOverwrite>
    <SqlDataFolder></SqlDataFolder>
    <SqlLogFolder></SqlLogFolder>
    <DisableSqlTriggers>Y</DisableSqlTriggers>
    <NonSysproCompId>N</NonSysproCompId>
    <AllowLogin>N</AllowLogin>
  </Options>
  <Include>
    <IncludeEsignatures>N</IncludeEsignatures>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="Windows-1252"?>
<!-- Copyright 1994-2022 SYSPRO Ltd.-->
<!-- 
     This is an example XML instance to demonstrate
     use of the Cluster Sync Manager Posting Business Object
	 Functions:
	   'FileUpload'        Upload one or more files from file system to sync manager
	   'ServerRegister'    Register one or more servers to sync manager
	   'ServerSetForced'   Set 'forced get' flag against selected or all servers
-->
<SyncManager xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsd:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="COMCLU.XSD">
  <Parameters>
    <Function>FileUpload</Function>

</SyncManager>

     ..\Plugin\CustomStore\DataId_?????? with the live data dictionary in SQL

COMTDC.sample_doc_xml:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="Windows-1252"?>
<!-- Copyright 1994-2022 SYSPRO Ltd.-->
<!-- 
     Sample XML for the SYSPRO Custom to Live Data Dictionary Merge business object.
     This business object will merge all custom data dictionaries stored in
     ..\Plugin\CustomStore\DataId_?????? into the SQL data dictionary
     Once completed the live data dictionary will be uploaded to SQL Server
     system-wide tables.
     Functions:
       M-Merge  (merges custom DD into SQL DD)
       D-Delete (deletes all custom entries from the live SQL data dictionary)
-->
<CustomDataDictionary xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsd:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="COMTDC.XSD">
  <Parameters>
    <Function>M</Function>
    <UploadToSql>Y</UploadToSql>
  </Parameters>
</CustomDataDictionary>

COMTPW.description: Sample XML Parameter for the Set Password Business Object The action type: "S" = setting a password for an operator that currently has no password configured. "OP" = changing a password for an operato
COMTPW.sample_input_xml:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="Windows-1252"?>
<!-- Copyright 1994-2019 SYSPRO Ltd.-->
<!-- 
    Sample XML Parameter for the Set Password Business Object 
    The action type: 
    "S" = setting a password for an operator that currently has no password configured. 
    "OP" = changing a password for an operator that has an existing password.
    "F" = send temporary password to operator that has forgotten their password.
    "P" = send temporary password to portal user that has forgotten their password. The email address is used to identify the operator and the operator filter is not used.
    "CP" = Changing a portal user password. The email address is used to identify the operator and the operator filter is not used.
-->
<PostCOMTPW xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsd:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="COMTPWDOC.XSD">
  <Password>begintext{operator}endtext</Password>
  <OldPassword>{provide existing password here}</OldPassword>
  <NewPassword>{provide new password here}

DDSTDC.sample_doc_xml:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="Windows-1252"?>
<!-- Copyright 1994-2022 SYSPRO Ltd.-->
<!-- 
     Sample XML for the SYSPRO Custom to Live Data Dictionary Merge business object.
     This business object will merge all custom data dictionaries stored in
     ..\Plugin\CustomStore\DataId_?????? with the live data dictionary in BASE\DD.
     Once completed the live data dictionary will be uploaded to SQL Server
     system-wide tables.
     Functions:
       M-Merge  (merges custom DD with live DD and upload to SQL)
       D-Delete (deletes all custom entries from the live data dictionary and upload to SQL)
     Defaults to uploading Live DD to SQL unless you pass 'N' to <UploadToSql>
-->
<CustomDataDictionary xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsd:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="DDSTDC.XSD">
  <Parameters>
    <Function>M</Function>
    <UploadToSql>Y</UploadToSql>
  </Parameters>
</CustomDataDictionary>

PORQOV.description: Sample XML for the Purchase Order Valuation Business Object
PORQOV.sample_input_xml:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="Windows-1252"?>
<!-- Copyright 1994-2010 SYSPRO Ltd.-->
<!-- 
     Sample XML for the Purchase Order Valuation Business Object
-->
<!-- To value an existing purchase order use this syntax:
<Query xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsd:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="PORQOV.XSD">  
  <Option>
    <PurchaseOrder>000219</PurchaseOrder>
  </Option>
  <XslStylesheet/>
</Query>
-->
<!-- 
    To value a potential purchase order supply an XML document in the following format:
 -->
<Query xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsd:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="PORQOV.XSD">  
  <Option>
    <OrderHeader>
      <Supplier>000001</Supplier>
      <ExchRateFixed>Y</ExchRateFixed>
      <ExchangeRate>2.50</ExchangeRate>
      <MulDiv>M</MulDiv>
      <Currency>$</Currency>
    </OrderHeader>
    <MerchandiseLine>
      <LineType>1</LineType>
      <MStockCode>B100</MStockCode>
      <MWarehouse>E</MWarehouse>
      <MOrderQty>100.000</MOrderQty>

SORQOV.description: This is an example XML instance to demonstrate use of the Sales Order Valuation Business Object This example passes the sales order details within the XML
SORQOV.sample_input_xml:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="Windows-1252"?>
<!-- Copyright 1994-2024 SYSPRO Ltd.-->
<!-- 
     This is an example XML instance to demonstrate
     use of the Sales Order Valuation Business Object
     This example passes the sales order details within the XML
-->
<!--
     To value an existing sales order use this syntax:<Query xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsd:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="SORQOV.XSD"><Option><SalesOrder>000812</SalesOrder><CalloutUsaTax>N</CalloutUsaTax><XslStylesheet/></Option></Query>
-->
<!--
    To value a potential sales order supply an XML document in the following format:
-->
<Query xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsd:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="SORQOV.XSD">
  <Option>
    <OrderHeader>
      <Customer>000001</Customer>

SYSQ02.description: Generic SQL SELECT Query - See the following notes Table - Up to 21 individual <Table> elements can be defined - Must start with alias ('a.' thru 'z.') - Maximum table name length is 32 including alia
SYSQ02.sample_input_xml:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="Windows-1252"?>
<!-- Copyright 1994-2017 SYSPRO Ltd.-->
<!-- 
Generic SQL SELECT Query - See the following notes
Table
- Up to 21 individual <Table> elements can be defined
- Must start with alias ('a.' thru 'z.')
- Maximum table name length is 32 including alias
- Must be defined under a <Tables> node
TableJoin
- For the second and subsequent <Table> elements you
  must define a <TableJoin> value immediately below
  the secondary table definition.
Column
- Up to 300 <Column> elements can be defined
- Must start with alias ('a.' thru 'z.')
- Maximum column name length is 32 including alias
- Must be defined under a <Columns> node
ColumnType
- Defines immediate prior 'Column' if not alpha
- N=Numeric column
- D=Date column
Where
- Maximum of 1000 characters to be added to WHERE clause
- Can contain up to 30 parameters (%1 thru %30)
- Consider using 'CDATA' if using '<' or '>'
WhereParam
- Defines values for parameters %1 thru %30
- Defaults to alpha values

SYSQLB.description: This is an example XML instance to demonstrate the use of the input XML for the internal business object used to test various RTS and other errors
SYSQLB.sample_input_xml:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="Windows-1252"?>
<!-- Copyright 1994-2022 SYSPRO Ltd.-->
<!-- 
     This is an example XML instance to demonstrate the use of the 
     input XML for the internal business object used to test various RTS and other errors
-->
<Query xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsd:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="SYSQLB.xsd">
  <!-- Function list
  RT1 - Create 240 error - object reference not valid
  RT2 - Create 173 error - program not found
  RT3 - Create 153 error - subscript out of range
  RT4 - Create 114 error - invalid memory allocation (can kill service process)
  RT5 - Create 24  error - Does not understand: unknownfunction
  RT6 - Create 33  error - Invalid parameter (eg -ve passed to character array length)
  MEM - Load multiple 1GB (approx) strings into memory - uses LoopCount element
  TM1 - Create temp files - does not delete them - LoopCount is number of files
  TM2 - Create temp file - wait time and then delete - LoopCount is time in seco
